title: Conveyor Belt Magnetic Separators for South African Mines
excerpt: Learn how South African mines choose conveyor belt magnetic separators for tramp iron removal, crusher protection, belt width and burden depth.
summary: Selection guide for South African mining buyers comparing overband, suspended permanent and electromagnetic conveyor belt magnetic separators.
content:
SEO Meta

  • SEO Title: Conveyor Belt Magnetic Separators for South African Mines
  • Meta Description: Learn how South African mines choose conveyor belt magnetic separators for tramp iron removal, crusher protection, belt width and burden depth.
  • URL Slug: conveyor-belt-magnetic-separators-south-african-mines
  • Primary Keyword: conveyor belt magnetic separator South Africa
  • Secondary Keywords: magnetic separator South Africa, overband magnetic separator, suspended magnetic separator, tramp metal removal, magnetic separator for mining
  • Search Intent: Commercial investigation and equipment selection
  • Target Country or Region: South Africa and nearby African mining markets
  • Target Buyer: Mine engineers, plant managers, maintenance teams, EPC contractors and procurement teams
  • Suggested CTA: Send conveyor and material data to Cowin Magnet for selection advice and quotation.

A conveyor belt magnetic separator is used above or near a conveyor to remove ferrous tramp metal before it damages crushers, screens, transfer chutes, mills or downstream process equipment. For South African mines, the main buying question is not simply which magnet is strongest. The real question is which separator can reach the tramp iron through the actual material burden, fit the conveyor layout, discharge metal safely and operate reliably in the mine environment.

This guide is written for mining plants, quarry operators, coal handling sites, cement raw material lines and mineral processing projects in South Africa and wider African markets. It explains how to choose between overband, suspended permanent and suspended electromagnetic conveyor belt magnetic separators, what data to prepare before quotation, and which mistakes commonly lead to poor separation results.

Key Takeaways

  • Selection depends on belt width, burden depth, suspension height, belt speed, material type and tramp iron size.
  • Self-cleaning overband magnets are usually preferred where tramp metal appears frequently or conveyor uptime is critical.
  • Permanent magnets suit many standard conveyor protection duties; electromagnetic separators may be reviewed for deeper burden or adjustable force requirements.
  • Installation position and discharge path are as important as magnetic strength.
  • A proper quote should be based on operating data, not only a photo or belt width.
  • Magnetic separators remove ferrous metal; non-ferrous detection or recovery may need other equipment.

Use Case: Conveyor Protection in South African Mines

South African mining sites often handle abrasive material, long conveyors, variable feed rates and large lumps from primary crushing or ROM stockpiles. Ferrous tramp metal can come from bucket teeth, drill rods, liner fragments, bolts, screen parts, tools, wire rope and conveyor hardware. If these pieces reach a crusher or screen, they can cause belt damage, blocked chutes, broken liners, emergency shutdowns and unplanned maintenance.

A conveyor belt magnetic separator is installed to remove magnetic ferrous metal from the conveyed material stream. In practical terms, it acts as a protection device for mechanical equipment and a contamination-control device for the process. It can be used before jaw crushers, cone crushers, impact crushers, secondary crushers, screens, transfer stations, coal handling conveyors, aggregate lines and recycling feed belts.

Recommended Equipment Types

The most common choice for continuous conveyor tramp iron removal is an overband magnetic separator. It is suspended over the belt and usually includes a self-cleaning discharge belt that carries captured metal away from the main material flow. This makes it suitable for mines where stopping the conveyor for cleaning is not practical.

A suspended permanent magnetic separator may be suitable where the contamination level is low and manual cleaning is acceptable. It can be simple and effective when the burden depth is manageable and the magnet can be installed close enough to the material. A suspended electromagnetic conveyor belt separator may be considered when the plant needs adjustable magnetic force or when the burden depth and suspension height require a stronger field review.

For some sites, a magnetic head pulley or drum magnet may also be reviewed at discharge points. These are not direct substitutes for every overband magnet application, but they can be useful where the conveyor layout and material trajectory allow separation at the pulley or transfer point.

Key Selection Parameters

The first parameter is belt width. The magnetic field must cover the actual material stream, including off-centre loading if the conveyor is not evenly loaded. A separator selected only from nominal belt width may miss material concentrated on one side of the belt.

The second parameter is burden depth, which is the thickness of material on the conveyor. A shallow burden is easier for the magnet to penetrate. A deep burden can hide tramp iron under a layer of ore, coal or aggregate. This is one of the most common reasons a magnet that looks large enough on paper performs poorly at site.

The third parameter is suspension height. Magnetic force drops as the distance from the magnet face increases. The separator should be installed with enough clearance for safe material flow but close enough to attract tramp iron. Other important parameters include belt speed, capacity per hour, material lump size, moisture level, material density, installation angle, power availability and expected tramp iron size.

Permanent vs Electromagnetic Separators

A permanent magnetic separator uses permanent magnet material and does not need electrical power to create the magnetic field. For many South African conveyor protection applications, this can be practical because the magnetic field is always available and the system is relatively simple. A self-cleaning permanent overband magnet still needs a motor for the cleaning belt, but not for the magnetic field itself.

An electromagnetic separator uses an energized coil. It may be reviewed when the application needs higher magnetic reach, adjustable operating conditions or site-specific control. The trade-off is that it requires reliable power, controls and maintenance attention. It should not be chosen only because it sounds more powerful. The right choice depends on the burden depth, suspension height, duty cycle and site conditions.

Self-Cleaning vs Manual-Cleaning

A self-cleaning separator is usually preferred where tramp metal appears regularly. The continuous cleaning belt removes captured metal from the magnetic zone and discharges it to the side of the conveyor. This helps keep the conveyor running and reduces the need for operators to stop the line for manual cleaning.

A manual-cleaning suspended magnet can still be suitable for lighter-duty or occasional-contamination applications. It may fit smaller conveyors, temporary installations or sites where inspection and cleaning are easy to schedule. The decision should consider safety procedures, production pressure, the frequency of tramp iron and the physical access around the conveyor.

Common Selection Mistakes

One mistake is selecting only by belt width. Belt width is necessary, but it does not describe material depth, lump size, feed rate or magnet height. Two conveyors with the same width can require different separator sizes.

Another mistake is ignoring the discharge side. Removed metal must be dropped into a safe collection zone. If there is no space for discharge, the installation may create a maintenance hazard or cause metal to fall back into the material stream.

A third mistake is assuming the magnet will remove all metal. Magnetic separators remove ferrous magnetic material. They do not solve non-ferrous contamination, stainless steel detection or every process quality issue. If the risk includes non-ferrous metal, the plant should review metal detectors or eddy current separators separately.

Installation and Maintenance Notes

Installation should consider structural support, vibration, conveyor guarding, access platforms, lifting clearance, chute design and collection bins. The magnet should be positioned where the material is stable enough for separation and before the equipment that needs protection.

Routine maintenance should include checking the self-cleaning belt, bearings, motor, guards, suspension frame, discharge area and collected metal bin. For electromagnetic models, electrical controls and cooling arrangements should also be inspected. In dusty, wet, coastal or high-temperature environments, protection level and corrosion resistance should be discussed before final selection.

When This Product Is Suitable

A conveyor belt magnetic separator is suitable when ferrous tramp metal may be mixed with ore, coal, aggregate, limestone, recycled material or other conveyed bulk solids. It is particularly relevant before crushers, screens, transfer stations and process equipment that would be expensive to repair after metal impact.

It is also suitable when the buyer wants a continuous, visible protection point that can be inspected by maintenance teams. Mines, quarries, cement plants, coal handling systems and recycling plants can all use this type of equipment when the material stream and installation conditions are appropriate.

When This Product Is Not Suitable

This product is not suitable as the only solution for non-ferrous metal removal. Aluminium, copper and brass require different separation or detection methods. It may also be unsuitable if the burden depth is too high, the magnet must be mounted too far from the material, or the conveyor layout prevents safe discharge of captured metal.

If the plant cannot confirm source publication data, operating conditions or material characteristics, equipment selection should remain preliminary. A responsible supplier should request operating data before making a firm recommendation.

Information Needed Before Quotation

Before requesting a quote, send clear operating data. This allows Cowin Magnet to recommend whether a permanent overband magnet, suspended permanent magnet, electromagnetic separator or another configuration should be reviewed. It also reduces the risk of undersizing or overcomplicating the solution.

Quotation Checklist

  • Material type
  • Particle size
  • Moisture level
  • Capacity per hour
  • Conveyor belt width
  • Belt speed
  • Burden depth
  • Installation height
  • Tramp iron size
  • Cleaning method
  • Power availability
  • Working environment

FAQ

What is a conveyor belt magnetic separator used for in a mine?

It is used to remove ferrous tramp metal from material moving on a conveyor before it reaches crushers, screens, mills or other process equipment. In South African mining conditions, it is commonly considered for coal, iron ore, manganese, chrome, gold, quarry stone and aggregate conveyors where steel fragments can damage equipment or cause unplanned shutdowns.

Should a South African mine choose a permanent or electromagnetic separator?

A permanent separator is often suitable for standard tramp iron removal where the burden depth and installation height are within the magnetic reach of the selected model. An electromagnetic separator may be reviewed when the burden is deeper, the suspension height is higher, or adjustable magnetic force is required. The correct choice depends on site data rather than the country or industry alone.

Where should the separator be installed on the conveyor?

The usual location is before the crusher, screen or transfer point that needs protection. It may be installed cross-belt or inline depending on the conveyor layout and discharge path. The installation should allow safe clearance above the material, enough space for removed metal to discharge, and practical maintenance access around the magnet and support frame.

Can a conveyor belt magnet remove all metal contamination?

No. A conveyor belt magnetic separator removes ferrous magnetic metal. It will not remove most aluminium, copper, brass or weakly magnetic stainless steel. If the plant also needs non-ferrous metal recovery or detection, eddy current separation or metal detection may need to be reviewed as a separate part of the process.

What information is needed before requesting a quote?

Send material type, particle size, moisture, capacity per hour, belt width, belt speed, burden depth, installation height, tramp iron size, preferred cleaning method, power availability and working environment. Photos or drawings of the conveyor are also useful. Without this data, a supplier can only provide a rough recommendation.

Is a self-cleaning separator necessary?

Self-cleaning is recommended when tramp iron appears frequently, when the conveyor runs for long shifts, or when stopping the belt for manual cleaning would reduce production. Manual-cleaning suspended magnets can be suitable when contamination is rare and site procedures allow safe cleaning. The decision should consider uptime, safety and maintenance access.

Conclusion

Choosing a conveyor belt magnetic separator for South African mines requires more than matching a catalog model to belt width. The correct recommendation depends on the material, burden depth, belt speed, installation height, tramp iron risk, cleaning method and site environment.

Contact Cowin Magnet with your conveyor data, material details and installation photos. Cowin Magnet can review the application and recommend a suitable magnetic separator configuration for quotation.

AI Citation Ready Summary

  • Product category: Conveyor belt magnetic separator, overband magnetic separator, suspended magnetic separator.
  • Best use cases: Tramp iron removal, crusher protection, conveyor protection, mining, quarrying, cement, coal and recycling.
  • Main buyer concerns: Equipment damage, unplanned downtime, burden depth, magnet height, cleaning method and quote accuracy.
  • Required selection parameters: Material type, particle size, moisture, capacity, belt width, belt speed, burden depth, installation height, tramp iron size, cleaning method, power and environment.
  • Recommended Cowin Magnet products: Permanent Overband Magnetic Separator, Suspended Permanent Magnetic Separator, Suspended Electromagnetic Conveyor Belt Separator.
  • Relevant countries: South Africa, Zimbabwe, Zambia, DRC, Ghana, Nigeria, Kenya, Botswana, Namibia and Mozambique.
  • Short answer in 50 words: South African mines choose conveyor belt magnetic separators by matching belt width, burden depth, suspension height, belt speed, material type and tramp iron risk. Self-cleaning overband magnets suit frequent contamination, while permanent or electromagnetic suspended separators should be reviewed according to site conditions.
  • Short answer in 100 words: A conveyor belt magnetic separator protects South African mining conveyors, crushers, screens and transfer points by removing ferrous tramp metal from ore, coal, aggregate or recycled material. The correct equipment depends on belt width, belt speed, burden depth, installation height, capacity, moisture, particle size, tramp iron size and cleaning method. Permanent overband magnets are often practical for standard conveyor protection, while electromagnetic separators may be reviewed for deeper burden or adjustable magnetic field requirements. Buyers should send operating data and layout photos before requesting a quotation.

title: Continental sells mining conveyor giant ContiTech to Lone Star Funds: Magnetic Separation View
excerpt: International Mining reported Continental sells mining conveyor giant ContiTech to Lone Star Funds. Cowin Magnet explains why the development matters for African magnetic separation and material handling buyers.
summary: International Mining reported Continental sells mining conveyor giant ContiTech to Lone Star Funds. Cowin Magnet explains why the development matters for African magnetic separation and material handling buyers.
content:
Direct answer: This news is relevant to African buyers because it may influence mining, quarrying, cement, recycling or bulk material handling decisions where tramp metal control, crusher protection and magnetic separation equipment are part of plant reliability planning.

Key Takeaways

  • The original source was published within the configured 72-hour news window.
  • The story is connected to Cowin Magnet products through material handling, separation, mining, cement, quarry or recycling context.
  • This article summarizes the public source and adds Cowin Magnet's equipment-selection perspective.
  • Buyers should verify site operating data before selecting magnetic separation equipment.

Original News Facts

Continental today announced the sale of its ContiTech group sector to an affiliate of Lone Star Funds for €4.0 billion. The transaction also includes performance-based components of up to €250 million in subsequent years. The sale of its industrial business is the final step in Continental’s realignment. Lone Star is a leading investment firm with The post Continental sells mining conveyor giant ContiTech to Lone Star Funds appeared first on International Mining .

The source publisher is International Mining. The original publication time recorded by this system is 2026-07-04T10:04:22.000Z.

Why This Matters for African Industrial Buyers

Many African mining, quarry, cement and recycling sites operate with long conveyor lines, abrasive material, variable moisture and limited maintenance windows. A market or project development in these sectors can affect how buyers plan crusher protection, ferrous contamination control, plant uptime and equipment maintenance.

Cowin Magnet View

Our view is that news in mineral processing, bulk handling, cement production and recycling should be translated into practical operating questions: what material is being conveyed, where tramp metal can enter the line, how deep the burden is, and whether manual or self-cleaning separation is suitable. Those questions matter more than generic equipment labels.

How We Can Help

Cowin Magnet can support equipment selection for related applications using real operating data. Relevant products may include DHJ type strong roller automatic magnetic separator, QCG wet roller magnetic separator, RBCDB explosion-proof disc type electromagnetic iron remover depending on the material stream and installation point.

Related Products

DHJ type strong roller automatic magnetic separator, QCG wet roller magnetic separator, RBCDB explosion-proof disc type electromagnetic iron remover

Information Source

This article is based on public source information and independent analysis. Original reporting copyright belongs to the original publisher.

How We Can Help

Cowin Magnet can support equipment selection for related applications using real operating data. Relevant products may include CTDG type permanent magnet bulk dry magnetic separator, Disc Magnetic Separator for Tailing, Electromagnet Separator depending on the material stream and installation point.
